incident_sources.label: Cyber Monitoring Centre — Category 2 classification (May 2025)
incident_sources.incentive_bias: regulator/independent body — floor not full picture
incident_sources.text: The Cyber Monitoring Centre has classified the Marks & Spencer and Co-operative Group cyber events of April–May 2025 as a single Category 2 systemic event, noting social engineering of IT help-desk processes as the disclosed initial access vector.

incident_sources.label: Marks & Spencer Group plc — LSE RNS announcement (22 Apr 2025)
incident_sources.text: Marks & Spencer confirms via LSE RNS that it is managing a cyber incident, that minor temporary changes were made to store operations, and that it has notified the NCSC and data protection authorities. The disclosure does not state the technical initial-access method and does not describe any customer-data exfiltration.

incident_sources.label: BleepingComputer — M&S NTDS.dit / ESXi reporting
incident_sources.text: Sources told BleepingComputer the attackers obtained the NTDS.dit database and encrypted VMware ESXi virtual machines, with the intrusion attributed to Scattered Spider.

incident_sources.label: Ransomware leak-site post claiming M&S data (May 2025)
incident_sources.incentive_bias: criminal actor-claim — excluded as victim-fact per registry §B8; admissible only as INFERRED-about-actor
incident_sources.text: A ransomware leak site has posted an entry claiming responsibility for the Marks & Spencer breach and asserting it exfiltrated large volumes of customer records, including specific named data categories. The post includes a purported file sample as proof. This content originates from the criminal actor's own infrastructure and is presented to pressure the victim.
